  1333→  Very subtle background gradient shift based on mouse position (±2px max
  1334→  movement). Creates depth illusion matching Raycast's glass effect. CSS
  1335→  custom properties updated via mousemove listener (throttled to rAF). Only
  1336→  affects background layer, not content. Disable when prefers-reduced-motion.
  1337→
  1338→=== SEARCH & INTERACTION ===
  1339→
  1340→- [x] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current
  1341→  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match
  1342→  characters don't need to be contiguous: "clhist" matches "Clipboard
  1343→  History". Score based on: consecutive matches (bonus), match at word
  1344→  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight
  1345→  matched characters in results (bold or underline). Create
  1346→  src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with score() and match() functions. Apply to
  1347→  root search and all view search bars.
  1348→  Done: Recursive-descent fuzzy scorer with greedy fallback when recursion
  1349→  cap (10k calls) is reached. Scoring: consecutive match (+8), word boundary
  1350→  (+10), first char (+12), case match (+1), position bonus (1-idx/len)*2,
  1351→  exact match (+50), gap penalties (-3 start, -1 per). Word boundary detection
  1352→  handles separators, camelCase, and digit-letter transitions. HighlightedText
  1353→  component accepts optional pre-computed indices to avoid double scoring.
  1354→  Uses <span> (not <mark>) for accessibility. SCSS uses $font-weight-semibold
  1355→  token. command_registry.ts searchCommands() scores and sorts by relevance.
  1356→  All 4 view search bars (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, Files) use fuzzyMatch
  1357→  for filtering and pass query to ListItem for highlighting. App.tsx root
  1358→  search passes query to ListItem. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
  1359→  Sub-tasks:
  1360→  - [x] SRCH001a - Create src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with fuzzyScore() and
  1361→    fuzzyMatch() functions. Score: consecutive match bonus, word boundary bonus,
  1362→    early position bonus, gap penalty. Return matched indices for highlighting.
  1363→  - [x] SRCH001b - Create HighlightedText component (src/components/highlighted_text/)
  1364→    that renders text with matched characters highlighted (bold). Takes text + query.
  1365→  - [x] SRCH001c - Update command_registry.ts searchCommands() to use fuzzy search
  1366→    with scoring and result sorting by score.
  1367→  - [x] SRCH001d - Update ListItem to accept optional query prop, use HighlightedText
  1368→    for title and subtitle rendering. Update App.tsx to pass query to ListItem.
  1369→  - [x] SRCH001e - Update all view search bars (ClipboardHistoryView,
  1370→    SnippetManagerView, EmojiPickerView, FileSearchView) to use fuzzy search
  1371→    for filtering and pass query for highlighting.
  1372→
  1373→- [x] [P1] SRCH002 - Add recent commands tracking. Track last 10 opened
  1374→  commands with timestamps. Show "Recent Commands" section in root search
  1375→  when query is empty (above other sections). Persist via localStorage/
  1376→  SQLite. When user opens a command (drill-in), add to recents. Deduplicate.
  1377→  Cap at 10. Create src/hooks/use_recent_commands.ts. Wire into App.tsx
  1378→  root search sections.
  1379→  Done: Created useRecentCommands hook (src/hooks/use_recent_commands.ts)
  1380→  following useFavorites pattern. localStorage persistence via storage.ts

file.content:
(close/minimize/maximize) on left side, centered title text. Make it
  draggable (data-tauri-drag-region). In web mode, hide title bar (browser
  has its own). Create src/components/title_bar/ with title_bar.tsx and
  title_bar.scss. Position above CommandPalette. 28px height matching macOS.

- [ ] [P2] VIS003 - Add smooth scroll momentum and snap-to-item. Lists
  should have native-feeling scroll momentum (CSS scroll-behavior: smooth).
  When scroll stops, snap to nearest item boundary (CSS scroll-snap-type:
  y proximity on list container, scroll-snap-align: start on items). Ensure
  keyboard navigation scrollIntoView uses smooth behavior. Test on macOS
  trackpad and mouse wheel.

- [ ] [P2] VIS004 - Add focus ring animation matching Raycast. When
  navigating list items with keyboard, show a subtle animated focus ring
  that transitions between items (not just instant highlight switch).
  Use CSS transition on a pseudo-element that moves between item positions.
  The ring should have a very subtle glow (box-shadow with accent color at
  10% opacity). 80ms transition duration. Only show on keyboard navigation,
  not mouse hover.

- [ ] [P3] VIS005 - Add window open/close animations. When CommandPalette
  appears: macOS-style spring animation (scale from 0.95 to 1.0, opacity
  0 to 1, with slight overshoot). When closing: scale to 0.97, opacity to 0,
  faster (150ms). Use CSS @keyframes with cubic-bezier easing that mimics
  spring physics. In Tauri mode, coordinate with window show/hide timing.

- [ ] [P3] VIS006 - Add Grid view hover preview tooltip. When hovering a
  GridItem for 800ms, show an enlarged preview tooltip above the item
  (larger icon/image, full title, metadata). Positioned above the item,
  centered. Fade in/out. Dismiss on mouse leave. Create
  src/components/grid/grid_preview.tsx and grid_preview.scss.

- [ ] [P3] VIS007 - Add subtle parallax effect to command palette background.
  Very subtle background gradient shift based on mouse position (±2px max
  movement). Creates depth illusion matching Raycast's glass effect. CSS
  custom properties updated via mousemove listener (throttled to rAF). Only
  affects background layer, not content. Disable when prefers-reduced-motion.

=== SEARCH & INTERACTION ===

- [x] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current
  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match
  characters don't need to be contiguous: "clhist" matches "Clipboard
  History". Score based on: consecutive matches (bonus), match at word
  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight
  matched characters in results (bold or underline). Create
  src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with score() and match() functions. Apply to
  root search and all view search bars.
  Done: Recursive-descent fuzzy scorer with greedy fallback when recursion
  cap (10k calls) is reached. Scoring: consecutive match (+8), word boundary
  (+10), first char (+12), case match (+1), position bonus (1-idx/len)*2,
  exact match (+50), gap penalties (-3 start, -1 per). Word boundary detection
  handles separators, camelCase, and digit-letter transitions. HighlightedText
  component accepts optional pre-computed indices to avoid double scoring.
  Uses <span> (not <mark>) for accessibility. SCSS uses $font-weight-semibold
  token. command_registry.ts searchCommands() scores and sorts by relevance.
  All 4 view search bars (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, Files) use fuzzyMatch
  for filtering and pass query to ListItem for highlighting. App.tsx root
  search passes query to ListItem. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] SRCH001a - Create src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with fuzzyScore() and
    fuzzyMatch() functions. Score: consecutive match bonus, word boundary bonus,
    early position bonus, gap penalty. Return matched indices for highlighting.
  - [x] SRCH001b - Create HighlightedText component (src/components/highlighted_text/)
    that renders text with matched characters highlighted (bold). Takes text + query.
  - [x] SRCH001c - Update command_registry.ts searchCommands() to use fuzzy search
    with scoring and result sorting by score.
  - [x] SRCH001d - Update ListItem to accept optional query prop, use HighlightedText
    for title and subtitle rendering. Update App.tsx to pass query to ListItem.
  - [x] SRCH001e - Update all view search bars (ClipboardHistoryView,
    SnippetManagerView, EmojiPickerView, FileSearchView) to use fuzzy search
    for filtering and pass query for highlighting.

- [x] [P1] SRCH002 - Add recent commands tracking. Track last 10 opened
  commands with timestamps. Show "Recent Commands" section in root search
  when query is empty (above other sections). Persist via localStorage/
  SQLite. When user opens a command (drill-in), add to recents. Deduplicate.
  Cap at 10. Create src/hooks/use_recent_commands.ts. Wire into App.tsx
  root search sections.
  Done: Created useRecentCommands hook (src/hooks/use_recent_commands.ts)
  following useFavorites pattern. localStorage persistence via storage.ts
  with "recent-commands" key. Max 10 items, most-recent-first ordering,
  deduplication on add. App.tsx integration: addRecent called only for
  commands with components (not detail-only or fallback items). Tray
  navigation also tracks recents. "Recent Commands" section shown in root
  search when query is empty, below Favorites, above registry sections.
  Items in Favorites excluded from Recent section. allFlatItems built from
  unfiltered registrySections to prevent filter-dependent disappearance.
  clearRecent exported for future UI integration. All 93 tests pass, bun
  run check passes.

  is empty, cycle through helpful placeholder texts every 4 seconds:
  "Type a command...", "Search applications...", "Calculate something...",
  "Find files...". Smooth fade transition between placeholders. Stop cycling
  when user starts typing. Resume on clear. Use CSS animation for opacity
  transitions. Update SearchBar component.

- [ ] [P2] SRCH004 - Add ⌘L to copy current item URL/value. Universal
  shortcut that copies the most relevant value of the selected item (URL

extracted from all components.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA012 - Add performance monitoring utilities. Create
  src/utils/perf.ts with: measureRender(componentName) HOC that logs
  render time in dev mode, useRenderCount() hook for detecting excessive
  re-renders, throttle() and debounce() utilities. Add React.StrictMode
  in dev. Profile List rendering with 500+ items. Identify and fix
  unnecessary re-renders with React.memo where needed. Target: 60fps
  scroll performance.

=== TESTING (NEW) ===

- [ ] [P1000] TEST007 - Add unit tests for calculator.ts. Test all math
  operations: basic arithmetic, operator precedence, parentheses, functions
  (sqrt, sin, cos, etc), constants (pi, e), percentages, "X% of Y" syntax,
  edge cases (division by zero, NaN, Infinity), invalid expressions, very
  large/small numbers. Minimum 30 test cases. Use vitest.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST008 - Add unit tests for fuzzy_search.ts (after SRCH001).
  Test scoring: exact match > prefix > word boundary > substring > fuzzy.
  Test highlighting of matched characters. Test edge cases: empty query,
  empty candidates, special characters, unicode. Minimum 20 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST009 - Add unit tests for storage.ts. Test get/set/remove
  with various types (string, number, boolean, object, array). Test
  validators (isStringArray, isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Test error
  handling (invalid JSON, quota exceeded mock). Test migration paths.
  Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST010 - Add unit tests for format_date.ts. Test all
  relative date ranges: just now, minutes ago, hours ago, yesterday, N days
  ago, absolute date, different year. Test edge cases: midnight boundaries,
  timezone handling, future dates, invalid dates. Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST011 - Add integration tests for command registry. Test
  register/get/search/getSections. Test search matches title, subtitle,
  keywords, aliases. Test section ordering. Test duplicate registration
  warning. Minimum 10 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST012 - Add E2E tests with Playwright for core flows.
  Flow 1: Open → type query → select result → drill into view → Escape back.
  Flow 2: Open → ⌘K → navigate actions → execute action → verify HUD.
  Flow 3: Settings → change theme → verify visual change.
  Flow 4: Clipboard History → pin item → verify pinned section.
  Each flow as separate test. Screenshot on failure.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST013 - Add accessibility audit tests. Use axe-core with
  Playwright to audit each view for WCAG 2.1 AA violations. Test: color
  contrast ratios, ARIA roles/labels, keyboard navigation order, focus
  management, screen reader announcements. Run on: root search, clipboard
  history, snippet manager, emoji picker, settings, forms. Zero violations
  target.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST014 - Add performance benchmark tests. Measure and assert:
  initial render time < 100ms, search filter time < 50ms for 1000 items,
  navigation push/pop < 100ms, scroll FPS > 55fps with 500 items. Use
  vitest + performance.now() for unit benchmarks. Use Playwright for
  runtime performance metrics. Create performance regression baseline.

- **[P1000]**: TEST001-TEST014

The highest priority pending task is **[P1] TAURI005** - Add native clipboard access via Tauri plugin. However, TAURI005/006/007 require actual native Tauri plugins and system-level access. Let me check if there's a more suitable P1 task.

Looking at all P1 tasks: TAURI005, TAURI006, TAURI007 (native desktop features), INFRA004 (extension architecture), INFRA009 (CI/CD). 

**INFRA004** and **INFRA009** are pure code tasks that can be done now. Let me pick **INFRA004** - Build extension architecture, as it's a structural improvement that benefits all future work.

But wait - let me also check INFRA003 (P2) which is about refactoring App.tsx - INFRA004 depends on understanding the current App.tsx structure. Let me scope INFRA004 first.

input.prompt:
Explore the codebase to understand the current command registry system and extension structure. I need to understand:

1. The current registry types in src/registry/types.ts
2. The current command_registry.ts implementation
3. The commands.ts file that registers all commands
4. The current src/registry/index.ts barrel export
5. What extension-like structure already exists (clipboard_history, snippet_manager, emoji_picker, etc.)
6. How App.tsx currently consumes the registry

Read all these files and provide a comprehensive summary of:
- Current types and their fields
- How commands are registered
- How views/components are referenced
- What would need to change for an extension manifest system
- List all existing "extension" directories under src/components/ that are self-contained views

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me provide a detailed summary of the current system:

## Comprehensive Summary of Current Command Registry and Extension Structure

### 1. Current Registry Types (`src/registry/types.ts`)

The `CommandRegistration` interface defines:
- `id` (string): Unique identifier for the command
- `name` (string): Display name
- `subtitle` (optional string): Secondary description
- `icon` (ReactNode): Component rendered as icon
- `keywords` (optional string[]): Search terms
- `aliases` (optional string[]): Alternative names for searching
- `section` (string): Category grouping ("Suggestions", "Commands", "Applications")
- `accessories` (optional ListItemAccessoryData[]): Right-side badges/metadata (tags, dates, text)
- `detail` (optional ListItemDetailData): Markdown content + metadata for detail view
- `component` (optional ComponentType): Full-view React component
- `fullView` (optional boolean): Whether component takes full screen

### 2. Command Registry Implementation (`src/registry/command_registry.ts`)

Uses a Map-based in-memory store:
- `registerCommand(cmd)`: Registers a command, tracks section order
- `getCommand(id)`: Retrieves single command
- `getSections()`: Returns all commands grouped by section
- `searchCommands(query)`: Fuzzy search across name, subtitle, keywords, aliases
- Maintains section order for consistent UI display
- Converts CommandRegistration to ListItemData for display

### 3. Commands Registration (`src/registry/commands.ts`)

Currently registers 19 commands:
- **Suggestions section** (full-view commands with components):
  - clipboard-history → ClipboardHistoryView
  - snippets → SnippetManagerView
  - file-search → FileSearchView
  - quicklinks → QuicklinksView
  - emoji → EmojiPickerView

- **Commands section** (mix of component + detail-only):
  - window-management → WindowManagementView
  - calculator (detail-only)
  - define-word (detail-only)
  - system-monitor (detail-only)
  - color-picker → ColorPickerView
  - settings → SettingsView

- **Applications section** (detail-only, no components):
  - app-safari, app-vscode, app-terminal, app-figma, app-slack

### 4. Barrel Export (`src/registry/index.ts`)

Exports:
```typescript
export { getCommand, getSections, searchCommands } from './command_registry';
export type { CommandRegistration } from './types';
// Side effect: imports './commands' to trigger registration
```

### 5. Core Type System (`src/types.ts`)

Supporting types used throughout:
- `ListItemData`: Title, subtitle, icon, accessories, detail
- `ListItemAccessoryData`: Text, icon, date, tag, tooltip
- `ListItemDetailData`: Markdown + metadata array
- `ListItemMetadataEntry`: Union type for label, link, tag-list, separator
- `SectionData`: Title + items array

### 6. Existing Self-Contained "Extension" Directories

All follow a two-file pattern (view component + scss):

| Directory | View Component | Status |
|-----------|---|---|
| `/src/components/clipboard_history/` | `clipboard_history_view.tsx` | Full-featured CRUD |
| `/src/components/snippet_manager/` | `snippet_manager_view.tsx` | Full-featured CRUD |
| `/src/components/emoji_picker/` | `emoji_picker_view.tsx` | Full-featured |
| `/src/components/file_search/` | `file_search_view.tsx` | Full-featured search |
| `/src/components/quicklinks/` | `quicklinks_view.tsx` | Full-featured CRUD |
| `/src/components/window_management/` | `window_management_view.tsx` | Layout visualization |
| `/src/components/color_picker/` | `color_picker_view.tsx` | Color selection |
| `/src/components/settings/` | `settings_view.tsx` | Configuration UI |

Additional component directories (not views):
- action_panel, alert, toast, search_bar, list, detail, kbd, form, icons, etc.

### 7. App.tsx Consumption

The App component uses the registry in several ways:

**Navigation System:**
- Maintains navigation stack with `NavViewData` type: either `command` (by ID) or `detail` (by ListItemData)
- `handleDrillIn()` logic:
  - If command has `component`: navigates to command view
  - Else if has `detail`: navigates to detail view
  - Otherwise: shows toast notification

**Rendering Strategy:**
```typescript
if (entry.data.type === 'command') {
  const cmd = getCommand(entry.data.commandId);
  if (cmd?.component) {
    const Component = cmd.component;
    return <Component />;
  }
}
if (entry.data.type === 'detail') {
  return <DetailView item={entry.data.item} />;
}
```

**Full-View Detection:**
```typescript
const isFullView = 
  currentNavData?.type === 'command' &&
  (getCommand(currentNavData.commandId)?.fullView ?? false);
```

When `fullView: true`, the SearchBar is hidden and component takes full screen.

**Tray Navigation:**
Maps OS tray events to specific commands via `TRAY_COMMAND_MAP`:
- clipboard → clipboard-history
- snippets → snippets
- settings → settings

### 8. Changes Required for Extension Manifest System

To support external extension manifests, the following would need to change:

1. **CommandRegistration**: Add optional `manifest` or `extensionId` field
2. **Registry**: Support dynamic registration from manifest files instead of hardcoded calls
3. **Type System**: Define manifest schema (with metadata, version, dependencies, etc.)
4. **Component Loading**: Runtime component resolution/lazy-loading from manifest paths
5. **Storage**: Persist extension enable/disable state
6. **Icon Handling**: Support both inline ReactNode and file path references
7. **Metadata**: Add extension author, version, source location fields
8. **Registry Index**: Load manifests from disk/config rather than explicit imports

All view components currently expect:
- React hooks (useState, useEffect, useCallback, etc.)
- Access to `/hooks/` (navigation, keyboard, alert, HUD, etc.)
- Access to `/components/` (common UI components)
- Access to `/utils/` (helpers like fuzzyMatch, storage, formatting)
- SCSS styling support

### 9. File Structure Pattern

Each extension view follows:
```
/src/components/[extension_name]/
  ├─ [extension_name]_view.tsx    (React component, ~15KB avg)
  └─ [extension_name]_view.scss   (Styling, ~1KB avg)
```

No subcomponents extracted—monolithic per extension for simplicity.

All absolute file paths are provided for reference:
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/types.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/command_registry.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/commands.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/index.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/App.tsx`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/types.ts`

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full scope. INFRA004 requires:

1. Create `ExtensionManifest` type in `src/registry/types.ts`
2. Create `src/extensions/` directory structure
3. Move each screen/extension into its own folder with manifest
4. Refactor registry to use extension manifests
5. Auto-discover extensions via barrel import

This is more than ~3 files, so I need to break it into sub-tasks. Let me plan the sub-tasks:

Sub-task A: Create ExtensionManifest type and update registry to support it
Sub-task B: Create extension manifests for built-in extensions (clipboard, snippets, emoji, etc.)


Sub-task C: Refactor commands.ts to use extension manifests
Sub-task D: Create barrel import for auto-discovery

Wait, let me reconsider the actual requirements here. The task is asking me to formalize each screen as an extension with a manifest containing id, name, icon, description, author, and commands. Each command needs name, title, subtitle, keywords, mode, and component. I should refactor the existing registry to use these manifests and organize everything under src/extensions/ with one folder per extension. The key is setting up the structure so extensions can be auto-discovered through a barrel import.

Breaking this into smaller pieces: first I need to define the ExtensionManifest and ExtensionCommand types, then create the extensions directory structure with individual manifests for each extension, and finally set up the barrel import for auto-discovery. The minimal approach is recognizing that the manifest is really just a structured wrapper around what's already in the registry—each extension declares its metadata and commands in one place, then gets loaded automatically. I realize these tasks are all tightly coupled together, so I'll consolidate them into one cohesive implementation with documented sub-tasks since each file will be small and follow a consistent pattern. Let me start building this out.

- [x] L03239 Setup Vite + React + TypeScript + Sass project scaffold.
     Install dependencies with bun: react, react-dom, @types/react,
     @types/react-dom, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, sass, eslint,
     @eslint/js, typescript-eslint, eslint-plugin-react-hooks,
     eslint-plugin-react-refresh, vitest, @testing-library/react,
     @testing-library/jest-dom, jsdom.
     Create vite.config.ts with React plugin and Sass support.
     Update tsconfig.json for JSX (react-jsx), DOM lib, paths.
     Create src/main.tsx entry point.
     Create index.html with root div.
     Create src/App.tsx placeholder component.
     Create eslint.config.js with TypeScript + React rules.
     Verify: bun run dev starts, bun run check passes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L0333 Create Sass design tokens file (src/styles/_tokens.scss).
     Define variables for: colors (background #1a1a1c, surface #262628,
     surface-hover #2e2e30, border #3a3a3c, text-primary #f5f5f7,
     text-secondary #98989d, text-tertiary #6e6e73, accent #007aff,
     accent-hover #0a84ff, destructive #ff453a),
     spacing (4px base scale: $space-1 through $space-8),
     border-radius ($radius-sm: 4px, $radius-md: 8px, $radius-lg: 12px,
     $radius-xl: 16px),
     shadows ($shadow-window, $shadow-popover),
     font stack (system sans-serif: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont,
     'SF Pro Text', 'Segoe UI', etc),
     font sizes ($font-xs: 11px, $font-sm: 12px, $font-md: 13px,
     $font-lg: 15px, $font-xl: 20px),
     transition durations ($transition-fast: 80ms, $transition-normal: 150ms,
     $transition-slow: 250ms).
     Match Raycast dark theme exactly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03450 Create global styles (src/styles/global.scss). Reset
     defaults, apply dark theme background, set font family,
     set box-sizing border-box, remove margins/padding. Import
     tokens. Style scrollbars to match Raycast (thin, subtle,
     webkit-scrollbar 6px, track transparent, thumb rgba white 0.15).
     Style ::selection with accent color. Set -webkit-font-smoothing
     antialiased. Set base font size and line height.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03459 Build root layout component (src/components/command_palette/).
     Full-screen centered container with frosted-glass card (the command
     palette window). Fixed width ~750px, dynamic height with max ~500px.
     Rounded corners ($radius-xl), subtle border (1px solid $border),
     drop shadow ($shadow-window). Background: $surface with slight
     transparency and backdrop-filter: blur. This is the main window
     that holds everything. Create command_palette.tsx and
     command_palette.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03488 Build SearchBar component (src/components/search_bar/).
     Input field at top of command palette. Magnifying glass icon on the
     left (SVG inline or icon component). Placeholder text "Search...".
     No visible border, bottom border-bottom 1px solid $border.
     Full width. Autofocus on mount. Expose onChange, value, placeholder
     props. Height ~46px. Font size $font-lg. Text color $text-primary.
     Placeholder color $text-tertiary. Create search_bar.tsx and
     search_bar.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03500 Build List component (src/components/list/). Vertical
     scrollable list below search bar. Accepts children (ListItem or
     ListSection). Keyboard navigation with arrow keys (up/down).
     Track active index with state. Scroll active item into view
     automatically (scrollIntoView). Overflow-y auto. Padding $space-1.
     Flex: 1 to fill remaining height. Create list.tsx and list.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03510 Build ListItem component (src/components/list/list_item/).
     Single row: icon (20x20) on left, title (primary text),
     optional subtitle (secondary text, $text-secondary). Right side
     shows accessories (shortcut badges, dates, status indicators).
     Height ~36px. Padding horizontal $space-3. Hover state with
     $surface-hover background. Active state (keyboard-selected) with
     $accent background and white text. onClick handler. Cursor pointer.
     Rounded corners $radius-md. Transition background $transition-fast.
     Create list_item.tsx and list_item.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03520 Build ListSection component (src/components/list/list_section/).
     Groups ListItems under a section header. Small uppercase label
     ($font-xs, $text-secondary, text-transform uppercase,
     letter-spacing 0.5px) with horizontal padding $space-3.
     Margin-top $space-2 (except first section). Used to categorize
     results like Raycast does (Applications, Commands, etc).
     Create list_section.tsx and list_section.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03530 Build ActionPanel component (src/components/action_panel/).
     Footer bar at bottom of command palette. Shows available actions
     for the selected item. Height ~36px. Background slightly different
     from main surface. Each action has a label and keyboard shortcut
     badge. Primary action on the left, secondary actions to the right.
     Top border 1px solid $border. Flex layout, align-items center.
     Padding horizontal $space-3. Create action_panel.tsx and
     action_panel.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03540 Build Kbd component (src/components/kbd/).
     Small inline element that renders a keyboard shortcut like
     "⌘K" or "↵". Styled as subtle bordered pill/box matching
     Raycast's shortcut indicators. Background slightly lighter than
     surface. Border 1px solid $border. Border-radius $radius-sm.
     Padding 1px 4px. Font-size $font-xs. Color $text-secondary.
     Display inline-flex, gap 2px. Used inside ActionPanel and ListItem.
     Create kbd.tsx and kbd.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03550 Build EmptyState component (src/components/empty_state/).
     Centered message shown when list has no results. Icon (large,
     48x48), title ($font-lg, $text-primary), and optional description
     ($font-sm, $text-secondary). Flex column, align-items center,
     justify-content center. Padding $space-8. Takes full available
     height. Create empty_state.tsx and empty_state.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03560 Build Detail component (src/components/detail/).
     Right panel view for showing detail content when an item is
     selected. Supports markdown-rendered content area with title,
     metadata rows (label/value pairs), and body text. Used in
     split-view layout where list is on left (~40% width) and detail
     on right (~60% width). Vertical scrollable. Padding $space-4.
     Border-left 1px solid $border. Create detail.tsx and detail.scss.
     Create detail_metadata.tsx for metadata sub-component with
     label, link, tag-list, and separator variants.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03570 Wire up demo data and compose full UI. Create mock data
     (fake commands, apps, bookmarks) in src/data/mock_data.ts.
     Define TypeScript interfaces for ListItemData, SectionData in
     src/types.ts. Render SearchBar + List with sections + ActionPanel
     together in App.tsx. Filter list items based on search input
     (case-insensitive substring match). Demonstrate keyboard
     navigation working end-to-end. Show Detail panel for selected
     item.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03580 Add transitions and micro-animations. Fade-in on mount
     for command palette (opacity 0->1, translateY slight).
     Smooth highlight transitions on list item hover/active
     (transition: background-color $transition-fast ease).
     Subtle scale on command palette appear (scale 0.98->1).
     Action panel slide-up entrance. Use CSS transitions only,
     no animation libraries. Define @keyframes in global.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03590 Polish and pixel-perfect review.

- [x] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current
  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match
  characters don't need to be contiguous: "clhist" matches "Clipboard
  History". Score based on: consecutive matches (bonus), match at word
  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight
  matched characters in results (bold or underline). Create
  src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with score() and match() functions. Apply to
  root search and all view search bars.
  Done: Recursive-descent fuzzy scorer with greedy fallback when recursion
  cap (10k calls) is reached. Scoring: consecutive match (+8), word boundary
  (+10), first char (+12), case match (+1), position bonus (1-idx/len)*2,
  exact match (+50), gap penalties (-3 start, -1 per). Word boundary detection
  handles separators, camelCase, and digit-letter transitions. HighlightedText
  component accepts optional pre-computed indices to avoid double scoring.
  Uses <span> (not <mark>) for accessibility. SCSS uses $font-weight-semibold
  token. command_registry.ts searchCommands() scores and sorts by relevance.
  All 4 view search bars (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, Files) use fuzzyMatch
  for filtering and pass query to ListItem for highlighting. App.tsx root
  search passes query to ListItem. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

content.thinking:
The critical issue: the `registerExtension` function generates IDs as `${manifest.id}-${cmd.name}`. This means:
- `clipboard-history-clipboard-history` instead of `clipboard-history`
- `snippets-snippets` instead of `snippets`
- `settings-settings` instead of `settings`
- `applications-app-safari` instead of `app-safari`

App.tsx references these IDs directly (e.g., `getCommand('clipboard-history')`, `getCommand('settings')`, `commandId: 'settings'`).

I have two options:
1. Use the command `name` directly as the ID (no prefix)


2. Update all references

Option 1 is simpler and preserves backward compatibility since command names are already unique. I'll modify `registerExtension` to use `cmd.name` as the ID directly.

content.text: The command IDs are referenced throughout App.tsx. I need to keep them stable. Let me update `registerExtension` to use `cmd.name` directly as the ID (the command names are already unique):

New registration order in `src/extensions/index.ts`:
1. clipboard (Suggestions)
2. snippets (Suggestions)
3. file_search (Suggestions)
4. quicklinks (Suggestions)
5. emoji (Suggestions)
6. window_management (Commands)
7. calculator (Commands)
8. dictionary (Commands)
9. system_monitor (Commands)
10. color_picker (Commands)
11. settings (Commands)
12. applications (Applications x5)

Section order preserved: Suggestions -> Commands -> Applications. Command count within sections: Suggestions=5, Commands=6, Applications=5. Matches.

Here is my review:

---

## PR Review: INFRA004 - Build Extension Architecture

### 1. Command ID Preservation -- PASS

All 16 command IDs are correctly preserved via `cmd.name` mapping to `id` in `registerCommand()`:

| Old ID | New `cmd.name` | Match |
|---|---|---|
| `clipboard-history` | `clipboard-history` | Yes |
| `snippets` | `snippets` | Yes |
| `file-search` | `file-search` | Yes |
| `quicklinks` | `quicklinks` | Yes |
| `emoji` | `emoji` | Yes |
| `window-management` | `window-management` | Yes |
| `calculator` | `calculator` | Yes |
| `define-word` | `define-word` | Yes |
| `system-monitor` | `system-monitor` | Yes |
| `color-picker` | `color-picker` | Yes |
| `settings` | `settings` | Yes |
| `app-safari` | `app-safari` | Yes |
| `app-vscode` | `app-vscode` | Yes |
| `app-terminal` | `app-terminal` | Yes |
| `app-figma` | `app-figma` | Yes |
| `app-slack` | `app-slack` | Yes |

All downstream references in `App.tsx` (lines 57, 61, 65, 69, 73, 569, 573, 577) use these IDs via `getCommand()` and will continue to resolve correctly.

### 2. `registerExtension` Implementation -- PASS (with notes)

The implementation at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/command_registry.ts` lines 16-34 is correct and clean. It iterates `manifest.commands`, falls back `cmd.section ?? manifest.name` for the section, and maps all fields properly. The field mapping is:

- `id` <- `cmd.name` (the command slug, used as registry key)
- `name` <- `cmd.title` (display name)
- `extensionId` <- `manifest.id` (traceability)

This naming is slightly confusing (`name` in `ExtensionCommand` is an ID slug, `name` in `CommandRegistration` is a display title), but it follows the Raycast manifest convention where `name` is a machine identifier and `title` is the human-readable label. Acceptable.

### 3. All 19 Commands Migrated -- PASS

The task description says "19 commands" but the old `commands.ts` has exactly **16** `registerCommand()` calls. The new manifests produce exactly **16** commands (11 single-command extensions + 1 five-command applications extension). All data is preserved byte-for-byte: keywords, aliases, accessories, detail metadata, sections, components, fullView flags.

Section ordering is also preserved. The `sectionOrder` array in `command_registry.ts` is populated by insertion order, and the registration order in `src/extensions/index.ts` matches the old file's order: Suggestions first, then Commands, then Applications.

### 4. Type Safety Issues -- TWO FINDINGS

**Finding 4a (Minor): `extensionId` is optional on `CommandRegistration`**

In `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/types.ts` line 42:
```typescript
extensionId?: string;
```

Since `registerExtension` always sets `extensionId`, and `registerCommand` is still publicly exported and could be called without it, the optional type is technically correct. However, if the intent is that all commands eventually come through extensions, this should be non-optional. As-is, this is fine for backward compatibility during the migration.

**Finding 4b (Low): No uniqueness guard on command IDs**

`registerCommand` does `commands.set(cmd.id, cmd)` which silently overwrites on collision. If two extensions accidentally declare a command with the same `name`, the second one wins silently. Consider adding a `console.warn` or a development-mode assertion:

```typescript
if (commands.has(cmd.id)) {
  console.warn(`Command ID "${cmd.id}" is already registered. Overwriting.`);
}
```

This is low priority since the current manifest set has no collisions, but it would prevent a nasty silent bug as extensions grow.

### 5. Architectural Concerns

**5a (Medium): Side-effect import chain is fragile**

`/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/index.ts` line 1:
```typescript
import '../extensions';
```

This is a bare side-effect import. The entire extension registration happens at module load time via import side effects: `registry/index.ts` -> `extensions/index.ts` -> each manifest import + `registerExtension()` call. This works, but:

- The import order in `extensions/index.ts` silently determines section ordering in the UI (because `sectionOrder` is a push-based array). Reordering imports during a refactor would change the UI with no type error or test failure.
- Tree-shaking tools may attempt to eliminate side-effect-only imports unless the bundler is configured to preserve them (Vite with Rollup generally handles this correctly for entry paths, but it is a known gotcha).

Recommendation: Add a brief JSDoc on `extensions/index.ts` documenting that import order = section order, so future contributors do not reorder imports.

**5b (Low): `icon` is `ReactNode` called at module scope**

Every manifest calls icon functions like `ClipboardIcon()` at module evaluation time (top-level const). This means all SVG icon components are instantiated when the app boots, regardless of whether they are ever rendered. For 16 commands this is negligible, but if the extension count grows to 100+, this becomes a boot-time cost. No action needed now, but worth noting for future architecture.

**5c (Positive): Clean separation of concerns**

Each extension is self-contained. Adding a new extension requires only creating a new directory with a `manifest.ts` and adding one line to `extensions/index.ts`. This is a good developer experience and matches the Raycast extension model well.

### 6. Data Integrity Verification

I compared every field of every command between the old `commands.ts` and the new manifests. All titles, subtitles, keywords, aliases, accessories, detail markdown, detail metadata, components, fullView flags, and sections are preserved exactly. TypeScript compiles cleanly with `--noEmit`.

### Summary

content.text:
Old order: Suggestions (5), Commands (6), Applications (5) = 16 total.

New registration order in `src/extensions/index.ts`:
1. clipboard (Suggestions)
2. snippets (Suggestions)
3. file_search (Suggestions)
4. quicklinks (Suggestions)
5. emoji (Suggestions)
6. window_management (Commands)
7. calculator (Commands)
8. dictionary (Commands)
9. system_monitor (Commands)
10. color_picker (Commands)
11. settings (Commands)
12. applications (Applications x5)

Section order preserved: Suggestions -> Commands -> Applications. Command count within sections: Suggestions=5, Commands=6, Applications=5. Matches.

Here is my review:

---

## PR Review: INFRA004 - Build Extension Architecture

### 1. Command ID Preservation -- PASS

All 16 command IDs are correctly preserved via `cmd.name` mapping to `id` in `registerCommand()`:

| Old ID | New `cmd.name` | Match |
|---|---|---|
| `clipboard-history` | `clipboard-history` | Yes |
| `snippets` | `snippets` | Yes |
| `file-search` | `file-search` | Yes |
| `quicklinks` | `quicklinks` | Yes |
| `emoji` | `emoji` | Yes |
| `window-management` | `window-management` | Yes |
| `calculator` | `calculator` | Yes |
| `define-word` | `define-word` | Yes |
| `system-monitor` | `system-monitor` | Yes |
| `color-picker` | `color-picker` | Yes |
| `settings` | `settings` | Yes |
| `app-safari` | `app-safari` | Yes |
| `app-vscode` | `app-vscode` | Yes |
| `app-terminal` | `app-terminal` | Yes |
| `app-figma` | `app-figma` | Yes |
| `app-slack` | `app-slack` | Yes |

All downstream references in `App.tsx` (lines 57, 61, 65, 69, 73, 569, 573, 577) use these IDs via `getCommand()` and will continue to resolve correctly.

### 2. `registerExtension` Implementation -- PASS (with notes)

The implementation at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/command_registry.ts` lines 16-34 is correct and clean. It iterates `manifest.commands`, falls back `cmd.section ?? manifest.name` for the section, and maps all fields properly. The field mapping is:

- `id` <- `cmd.name` (the command slug, used as registry key)
- `name` <- `cmd.title` (display name)
- `extensionId` <- `manifest.id` (traceability)

This naming is slightly confusing (`name` in `ExtensionCommand` is an ID slug, `name` in `CommandRegistration` is a display title), but it follows the Raycast manifest convention where `name` is a machine identifier and `title` is the human-readable label. Acceptable.

### 3. All 19 Commands Migrated -- PASS

The task description says "19 commands" but the old `commands.ts` has exactly **16** `registerCommand()` calls. The new manifests produce exactly **16** commands (11 single-command extensions + 1 five-command applications extension). All data is preserved byte-for-byte: keywords, aliases, accessories, detail metadata, sections, components, fullView flags.

Section ordering is also preserved. The `sectionOrder` array in `command_registry.ts` is populated by insertion order, and the registration order in `src/extensions/index.ts` matches the old file's order: Suggestions first, then Commands, then Applications.

### 4. Type Safety Issues -- TWO FINDINGS

**Finding 4a (Minor): `extensionId` is optional on `CommandRegistration`**

In `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/types.ts` line 42:
```typescript
extensionId?: string;
```

Since `registerExtension` always sets `extensionId`, and `registerCommand` is still publicly exported and could be called without it, the optional type is technically correct. However, if the intent is that all commands eventually come through extensions, this should be non-optional. As-is, this is fine for backward compatibility during the migration.

**Finding 4b (Low): No uniqueness guard on command IDs**

`registerCommand` does `commands.set(cmd.id, cmd)` which silently overwrites on collision. If two extensions accidentally declare a command with the same `name`, the second one wins silently. Consider adding a `console.warn` or a development-mode assertion:

```typescript
if (commands.has(cmd.id)) {
  console.warn(`Command ID "${cmd.id}" is already registered. Overwriting.`);
}
```

This is low priority since the current manifest set has no collisions, but it would prevent a nasty silent bug as extensions grow.

### 5. Architectural Concerns

**5a (Medium): Side-effect import chain is fragile**

`/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/registry/index.ts` line 1:
```typescript
import '../extensions';
```

This is a bare side-effect import. The entire extension registration happens at module load time via import side effects: `registry/index.ts` -> `extensions/index.ts` -> each manifest import + `registerExtension()` call. This works, but:

- The import order in `extensions/index.ts` silently determines section ordering in the UI (because `sectionOrder` is a push-based array). Reordering imports during a refactor would change the UI with no type error or test failure.
- Tree-shaking tools may attempt to eliminate side-effect-only imports unless the bundler is configured to preserve them (Vite with Rollup generally handles this correctly for entry paths, but it is a known gotcha).

Recommendation: Add a brief JSDoc on `extensions/index.ts` documenting that import order = section order, so future contributors do not reorder imports.

**5b (Low): `icon` is `ReactNode` called at module scope**

Every manifest calls icon functions like `ClipboardIcon()` at module evaluation time (top-level const). This means all SVG icon components are instantiated when the app boots, regardless of whether they are ever rendered. For 16 commands this is negligible, but if the extension count grows to 100+, this becomes a boot-time cost. No action needed now, but worth noting for future architecture.

**5c (Positive): Clean separation of concerns**

Each extension is self-contained. Adding a new extension requires only creating a new directory with a `manifest.ts` and adding one line to `extensions/index.ts`. This is a good developer experience and matches the Raycast extension model well.

### 6. Data Integrity Verification

I compared every field of every command between the old `commands.ts` and the new manifests. All titles, subtitles, keywords, aliases, accessories, detail markdown, detail metadata, components, fullView flags, and sections are preserved exactly. TypeScript compiles cleanly with `--noEmit`.

### Summary

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Command IDs preserved | Pass |
| `registerExtension` correctness | Pass |
| All commands migrated | Pass (16/16) |
| Type safety | Pass (2 minor notes) |
| Architecture | Pass (1 medium note on import ordering) |
| Data integrity | Pass |
| TypeScript compilation | Pass |

**Verdict: Approve with minor suggestions.** The two actionable items are:

1. Add a duplicate-ID guard in `registerCommand` (defensive, prevents silent bugs).
2. Document that import order in `extensions/index.ts` determines section display order.

Neither blocks merge.

Good review. Let me address both suggestions:

1. Add duplicate-ID guard in `registerCommand`
2. Not adding a JSDoc comment about import order (project rules say no comments unless JSDoc for public API)

  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match
  characters don't need to be contiguous: "clhist" matches "Clipboard
  History". Score based on: consecutive matches (bonus), match at word
  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight
  matched characters in results (bold or underline). Create
  src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with score() and match() functions. Apply to
  root search and all view search bars.
  Done: Recursive-descent fuzzy scorer with greedy fallback when recursion
  cap (10k calls) is reached. Scoring: consecutive match (+8), word boundary
  (+10), first char (+12), case match (+1), position bonus (1-idx/len)*2,
  exact match (+50), gap penalties (-3 start, -1 per). Word boundary detection
  handles separators, camelCase, and digit-letter transitions. HighlightedText
  component accepts optional pre-computed indices to avoid double scoring.
  Uses <span> (not <mark>) for accessibility. SCSS uses $font-weight-semibold
  token. command_registry.ts searchCommands() scores and sorts by relevance.
  All 4 view search bars (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, Files) use fuzzyMatch
  for filtering and pass query to ListItem for highlighting. App.tsx root
  search passes query to ListItem. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [ ] [P2] INFRA007 - Add state management with @belt/tools state-machine.
Replace prop-drilling and scattered useState across App - belt/tools is an
internal package public available in npm. See docs and examples at /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools

- [ ] [P2] INFRA008 - Add proper build configuration for production. Vite
  production build: minification (terser), tree-shaking, chunk splitting
  (vendor chunk for React, component chunks per route/view). Configure
  asset hashing for cache busting. Add bundle size analysis (rollup-plugin-
  visualizer). Set up path aliases (@/ for src/). Add environment variable
  support (.env, .env.production). Target: initial bundle < 200KB gzipped.
  Update vite.config.ts.

- [ ] [P1] INFRA009 - Add CI/CD pipeline. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml:
  lint (bun run check), test (bun run test), build (bun run build), optional
  Playwright visual tests. Run on push to main and PRs. Cache bun
  dependencies. Fail on lint errors or test failures. Add status badges
  to README. For Tauri: add tauri-build job that creates platform-specific
  binaries (macOS .dmg, Windows .msi, Linux .AppImage).

- [ ] [P3] INFRA010 - Add telemetry/analytics abstraction. Create
  src/utils/analytics.ts with track(event, properties?) function. Events:
  command_opened, search_query, action_triggered, extension_enabled/disabled,
  theme_changed. In web demo: log to console. In production: send to
  analytics endpoint. Respect user opt-out preference in settings. Add
  "Share Usage Data" toggle in Settings > Advanced. No PII in events.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA011 - Add i18n internationalization support. Create
  src/i18n/ with translation files (en.json, pt.json, es.json, ja.json).
  Translation keys for all UI strings (button labels, section titles,
  placeholder texts, error messages). Create useTranslation hook that
  returns t(key) function. Language selector in Settings > General. Default
  to system locale. Persist preference. Start with English strings
  extracted from all components.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA012 - Add performance monitoring utilities. Create
  src/utils/perf.ts with: measureRender(componentName) HOC that logs
  render time in dev mode, useRenderCount() hook for detecting excessive
  re-renders, throttle() and debounce() utilities. Add React.StrictMode
  in dev. Profile List rendering with 500+ items. Identify and fix
  unnecessary re-renders with React.memo where needed. Target: 60fps
  scroll performance.

=== TESTING (NEW) ===

- [ ] [P1000] TEST007 - Add unit tests for calculator.ts. Test all math
  operations: basic arithmetic, operator precedence, parentheses, functions
  (sqrt, sin, cos, etc), constants (pi, e), percentages, "X% of Y" syntax,
  edge cases (division by zero, NaN, Infinity), invalid expressions, very
  large/small numbers. Minimum 30 test cases. Use vitest.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST008 - Add unit tests for fuzzy_search.ts (after SRCH001).
  Test scoring: exact match > prefix > word boundary > substring > fuzzy.
  Test highlighting of matched characters. Test edge cases: empty query,
  empty candidates, special characters, unicode. Minimum 20 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST009 - Add unit tests for storage.ts. Test get/set/remove
  with various types (string, number, boolean, object, array). Test
  validators (isStringArray, isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Test error
  handling (invalid JSON, quota exceeded mock). Test migration paths.
  Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST010 - Add unit tests for format_date.ts. Test all
  relative date ranges: just now, minutes ago, hours ago, yesterday, N days
  ago, absolute date, different year. Test edge cases: midnight boundaries,
  timezone handling, future dates, invalid dates. Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST011 - Add integration tests for command registry. Test
  register/get/search/getSections. Test search matches title, subtitle,
  keywords, aliases. Test section ordering. Test duplicate registration
  warning. Minimum 10 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST012 - Add E2E tests with Playwright for core flows.
  Flow 1: Open → type query → select result → drill into view → Escape back.
  Flow 2: Open → ⌘K → navigate actions → execute action → verify HUD.
  Flow 3: Settings → change theme → verify visual change.
  Flow 4: Clipboard History → pin item → verify pinned section.
  Each flow as separate test. Screenshot on failure.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST013 - Add accessibility audit tests. Use axe-core with
  Playwright to audit each view for WCAG 2.1 AA violations. Test: color
  contrast ratios, ARIA roles/labels, keyboard navigation order, focus
  management, screen reader announcements. Run on: root search, clipboard
  history, snippet manager, emoji picker, settings, forms. Zero violations
  target.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST014 - Add performance benchmark tests. Measure and assert:
  initial render time < 100ms, search filter time < 50ms for 1000 items,
  navigation push/pop < 100ms, scroll FPS > 55fps with 500 items. Use
  vitest + performance.now() for unit benchmarks. Use Playwright for
  runtime performance metrics. Create performance regression baseline.
